arts.ttl: Stoke City team news vs Hull as Mark Robins makes four changes
arts.sum: Tatsuki Seko, Bae Junho, Josh Wilson-Esbrand and Al-Hamadi come into the starting XI while Junior Tchamadeu, Andrew Moran and Lewis Koumas drop to the bench. We’ve got to try to entertain people, keep them coming and wanting to come.

arts.ttl: Hull vs Stoke City live - Team news from MKM Stadium - Stoke-on-Trent Live
arts.sum: Mark Robins is heading into his second month as Stoke manager looking to build on a first few weeks that have seen one win, three draws and one defeat, with the victory coming in the FA Cup third round at Sunderland. Hull manager Ruben Selles has been in situ for about three more weeks than Robins and has started to see an upturn in fortunes with four wins in eight games before this weekend.

arts.ttl: Every player linked with Stoke City so far in challenging transfer window
arts.sum: Stoke were linked with the Birmingham captain Krystian Bielik but he is not a player who is believed to have been up for discussion this month, either leaving his current club or going elsewhere. The 32-year-old has already been linked with interest Blackburn Rovers, Alex Neil's Millwall and Preston North End. Schlupp is also reported to be liked by Major League Soccer (MLS) clubs as well, including San Diego FC and Charlotte FC.

arts.ttl: Stoke City old boy joins Championship rivals as outgoing striker primed for Nathan Jones showdown
arts.sum: The 22-year-old midfielder has signed for Alex Neil for an undisclosed fee widely reported to be about £1 million, a huge chunk less than Stoke City were quoted when they inquired with Wolves about turning a loan deal permanent last summer. Steve Gallen, Millwall's director of football, said: “Luke has Championship and some Premier League experience – he is a midfielder who is comfortable on the ball, is full of energy and wants to run and play forward.

arts.ttl: Eliot Matazo has different view on Hull City and it's up to him to prove why
arts.sum: Fresh into the group, Matazo admits the lowly league position has caught him by surprise, but believes Selles' squad has the quality to pull away from danger, providing everybody does their bit. Matazo joined City from Monaco last week in one of the more eye-catching moves in the Championship and came off the bench to make his debut at Sheffield United last Friday evening when Ruben Selles' side produced a terrific display to win 3-0 and stun Bramall Lane.

arts.ttl: Hull City's time has come to banish MKM Stadium woes and grasp survival chance
arts.sum: Even Plymouth Argyle, who are starting to get cut adrift at the foot of the table, have mustered four wins from their 14 home outings, and it's something City must find a cure for in the remaining weeks if they're to escape the clutches of the Championship's nether regions, which, given the players at their disposal, must surely happen? Tim Walter couldn't halt City's dreadful form at the MKM Stadium and while Ruben Selles has got the Tigers upwardly mobile, results at home have still not been anywhere near where they need to be.

arts.ttl: Stoke City chief Mark Robins comments on Hull City's January transfer strategy
arts.sum: Stoke City manager Mark Robins has praised the work Hull City have done in the transfer market after making Louie Barry their seventh arrival of a busy January window so far. “They’ve changed things around and brought good players in, Louie Barry being one of those.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins opens up on Stoke City goal problem, entertainment and Hull signing Louie Barry
arts.sum: Robins isn’t playing down the seriousness of it all, especially against a Hull team they are level on points at the wrong end of the Championship this weekend (Saturday, 3pm), but it’s all about trying to consistently improve performance levels that will, in turn, lead to consistently better results. “For us going there it’s an opportunity for us to put in a good performance and I’m looking for that really, looking to try to move things forward in terms of the performance levels.

arts.ttl: What Hull boss Ruben Selles is expecting from Stoke City under Mark Robins
arts.sum: Hull boss Ruben Selles is braced to take on ‘the best version of Stoke City’ since Mark Robins arrived at the club after a free week on the training ground. "I have been there myself when you pick a team and you are the third manager in the season, it's never easy, but I think he's getting some performances trying to put the team together and with the players (signed) in the transfer window, he can get his team stronger and go in the way that he wants to go as he did it in his previous club (at Coventry City).

arts.ttl: Mark Robins on 'powerful' Ali Al-Hamadi's first week at Stoke ahead of debut
arts.sum: The 22-year-old is primed to make his Stoke debut this weekend at Hull after signing on loan from Ipswich, where he has been largely to try to influence games as ‘a finisher’ substitute as they won promotion last term and in the Premier League this season. This is something that he wanted to get into his armoury just to make sure he’s got that ability to start and finish games.
